messages.content: You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Should we bubble the error up instead of alter conversion strategy?
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

You are reviewing a Go pull request. Here is a change in `inventory.go`:
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,105 @@
+package create
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"strings"
+
+	"github.com/ollama/ollama/x/safetensors"
+)
+
+// SourceTensor describes one tensor found in a source model: its on-disk type
+// and shape and which safetensors file holds it. It carries no weight data —
+// only what the header and shard index reveal.
+type SourceTensor struct {
+	Name  string
+	Dtype string
+	Shape []int32
+	File  string // safetensors file basename, relative to the model directory
+}
+
+// Inventory is the immutable result of reading a source model: every tensor
+// indexed by name, plus the parsed config and the model directory. Reading
+// source headers happens only here; the classify, plan, and write steps work
+// entirely from this listing and never re-open a source header to make a
+// decision.
+type Inventory struct {
+	Dir     string
+	Config  sourceModelConfig
+	Tensors map[string]SourceTensor
+}
+
+// Has reports whether a tensor with the given name exists in the source.
+func (inv Inventory) Has(name string) bool {
+	_, ok := inv.Tensors[name]
+	return ok
+}
+
+// ReadInvento
Since the names get some amount of normalization, does it make sense to detect name conflicts across multiple config files and error with a clear message that we wound up generating a clashing name?
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

You are reviewing a Go pull request. Here is a change in `blockfp8.go`:
```diff
@@ -0,0 +1,173 @@
+package create
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"slices"
+	"sort"
+	"strings"
+)
+
+// planBlockFP8 plans an HF block-FP8 source. MLX has no FP8 tensor type, so
+// every FP8 weight is decoded to BF16 using its block scale and then quantized
+// to the target (mxfp8); a weight the policy declines is still decoded and kept
+// at BF16 (it is never stored as FP8). BF16 tensors in the source are quantized
+// per the policy or kept at source precision (norms stay BF16/F32).
+func planBlockFP8(inv Inventory, target string, policy quantizePolicy) ([]BlobSpec, error) {
+	// The scale companion of each FP8 weight is folded into that weight's
+	// blob, so it is not emitted on its own.
+	consumed := make(map[string]bool)
+	for _, name := range sortedTensorNames(inv) {
+		if isFP8Weight(inv, name) {
+			if scale, ok := fp8ScaleFor(inv, name); ok {
+				consumed[scale] = true
+			}
+		}
+	}
+
+	groups := make(map[string][]SourceTensor)
+	fp8Groups := make(map[string][]SourceTensor)
+	specs := make([]BlobSpec, 0, len(inv.Tensors))
+	for _, name := range sortedTensorNames(inv) {
+		if consumed[name] {
+			c
I think if we had a missing scale, the error path would be subtly missed and we'd fall through and treat the tensor as a normal tensor instead of bailing out clearly with an error.
